lines.target: Learn this lesson as usual, then answer the following questions with the help of the preceding lesson.
lines.base: Apréndase esta lección como de costumbre y luego responda a las preguntas siguientes con la ayuda de la lección precedente.

lines.target: How much did David have to pay the doctor when he was ill?
lines.base: ¿ Cuánto tuvo que pagar David al médico cuando estuvo enfermo?

lines.target: What does National Health Service mean?
lines.base: ¿ Que quiere decir « National Health Service » ?

lines.target: How is the National Health Service financed?
Whose responsibility is the weekly contribution?
lines.base: ¿De quién es [la] responsabilidad [de] la contribución semanal ?

lines.target: Who has to make National Insurance contributions?
lines.base: ¿ Quien tiene que contribuir al Seguro Nacional ?
¿ Paga algo el que da empleo?

lines.target: Two little boys who were visiting the British Museum stopped to look at an Egyptian mummy.
lines.base: Dos chicos pequeños que visitaban el British Museum se pararon a mirar una momia egipcia.

lines.target: The mummy was covered with bandages and had a sign with 1215 B.C. (one thousand two hundred and fifteen) around its neck.
lines.base: La momia estaba cubierta de vendas y llevaba un letrero (signo) alrededor del cuello que decía «1215 B.C. ».

lines.target: I wonder what that sign means, said one of the boys.
lines.base: Me pregunto que significa ese letrero, dijo uno de los muchachos.

lines.target: That must be the number of the car which knocked him over, replied his friend.
lines.base: Debe ser el número [de matrícula] del coche que le atropelló, respondió su amigo.

gap_candidates.target: What does National Health Service mean?
How is the National Health Service financed?
Whose responsibility is the weekly contribution? (1)
Who has to make National Insurance contributions?
Two little boys who were visiting the British Museum stopped to look at an Egyptian mummy.
The mummy was covered with bandages and had a sign with 1215 B.C. (one thousand two hundred and fifteen) around its neck. (2) (3)
I wonder what that sign means, said one of the boys. (4)
